name: Mantling
description: A ledge recovery action that detects a valid edge, snaps the character to it, and moves them onto the upper surface through scripted or root-motion alignment.
design_notes: Mantling is a reliability mechanic: it turns almost-made jumps into readable recoveries. Define ledge_id detection, approach angle, clearance height, snap position, hand placement, camera transition, and cancellation rules. It should rescue intended traversal without letting players climb every decorative lip or bypass locked paths.
design_purpose: Smooth out ledge recovery when jumps, vaults, or climbs reach a valid upper surface.
Let level designers author readable vertical routes without requiring perfect landing precision.
Bridge movement systems such as jumping, climbing, parkour vaults, and crouch spaces.
player_fantasy: The player feels physically capable of catching a ledge and pulling over it instead of bouncing off geometry.

parameters.name: mantle_snap_distance_units
parameters.description: Maximum correction distance from character to ledge snap position.

parameters.name: mantle_clearance_height_units
parameters.description: Required empty space above the ledge landing position.

parameters.name: mantle_approach_angle_degrees
parameters.description: Allowed angle between character movement and ledge normal.

parameters.name: mantle_window_ms
parameters.description: Committed motion interval for the mantle transition.

parameters.name: mantle_motion_profile_id
parameters.description: Animation or scripted movement profile selected for the ledge height.

parameters.name: mantle_exit_probe_units
parameters.description: Forward clearance probe used before placing the character on top.

edge_cases: The ledge can be valid from the camera ray but blocked by collision at the landing point.
Moving platforms need ledge_snap_position relative to the platform, not only world coordinates.
A player may request mantle while carrying objects, aiming, knocked back, or under stamina exhaustion.
Networked mantle requires the same ledge_id and root_motion_policy_id on client and server.
common_bugs: Mantle starts from a decorative edge with no safe landing surface.
Root motion pushes the character through collision because the sweep is disabled during the montage.
Camera clips into the ledge when vertical correction is large.
Canceling mantle leaves input disabled or character gravity off.
balancing_notes: Wide snap distance makes traversal forgiving but can feel magnetic around unintended ledges.
Auto-mantle improves flow in action games but can steal player control near hazards or stealth cover.
High ledges should communicate whether mantle, climb, or wall jump is expected.
accessibility_notes: Expose optional auto-mantle or extended input buffering for players who struggle with precise ledge timing.
Avoid sudden camera lifts during mantle, or provide reduced camera motion settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Use forward and downward shape casts to produce ledge_id, ledge_snap_position, and mantle_clearance_height_units. During mantle, scripted motion or root motion should still sweep the capsule and abort to a safe fallback if landing clearance changes.
implementation_notes.godot: Use RayCast or ShapeCast nodes for ledge and exit probes, then lock traversal_state_id while applying a deterministic mantle motion profile in _physics_process. Restore gravity and input through a single exit function.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use trace profiles, Motion Warping, or root motion sources with a validated target transform. Server should approve ledge_id and landing clearance before clients commit to the mantle montage.
implementation_notes.web: Store mantle_state with ledge id, snap position, motion profile, and remaining ticks. Move the capsule through swept segments and revalidate exit space before returning to normal locomotion.

example_games.title: Uncharted 4
example_games.note: Uses mantle-style ledge recovery during authored traversal routes.

example_games.title: Assassin's Creed II
example_games.note: Uses ledge traversal and climb-over actions as a core navigation layer.
